name: Neighbors of Watertown, INC.
description_short: Non-profit housing and community development organization in Watertown, NY offering HUD-approved housing counseling, first-time homebuyer assistance, and residential rehabilitation programs.
description_long:
Neighbors of Watertown, Inc. is a community development organization based in Watertown, New York that focuses on neighborhood revitalization and housing support. Founded to serve the Watertown community, the organization operates from 112 Franklin Street and maintains extended business hours (Monday-Friday, 8am-5pm) to accommodate residents seeking assistance.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of housing-related services including HUD-approved housing counseling, first-time homebuyer education programs, down payment grant funding for qualified homeowners, and residential rehabilitation assistance at both city and county levels. They provide free financial counseling to residents experiencing economic stress, help with mortgage and creditor communication, credit protection guidance, and scam avoidance education. Additionally, Neighbors of Watertown manages rental properties, offers online rent payment solutions, maintains commercial real estate listings, and operates a buy/rehab/resell housing program.

What distinguishes this organization is its dual focus on both direct financial counseling and physical community development. Beyond counseling services, they actively rehabilitate historic properties through their Main Street program focused on the Public Square National Register Historic District, manage 16+ residential apartment complexes, and maintain commercial properties available for lease. Their first-time homebuyer grants are specifically available for homes purchased within Watertown city limits, providing direct financial assistance rather than guidance alone.

As a non-profit free-help resource, Neighbors of Watertown is best suited for Watertown residents facing housing instability, prospective homebuyers lacking down payment resources, renters needing financial counseling during economic hardship, and homeowners seeking rehabilitation financing. The primary limitation is geographic—services appear focused on the Watertown, New York area and Jefferson County, making this resource valuable only for residents in that specific region.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselors available for free financial and housing counseling consultations
Down payment grants specifically available for first-time homebuyers purchasing homes in Watertown
Multiple housing rehabilitation programs available at city and county levels for owner-occupied properties
Free homebuyer education courses scheduled for 2026 with no apparent cost barriers
Assistance with mortgage lender and creditor contact/negotiation during financial hardship
Online rent payment option available with staff support via phone
Non-profit status ensures mission-driven service rather than profit-driven lending
Extended business hours (8am-5pm weekdays) with direct contact to named counselor (Angelica)
cons: Services appear geographically limited to Watertown, NY and Jefferson County area—not accessible to consumers outside this region
Website lacks specific details about grant amounts, income limits, or eligibility criteria for down payment assistance
No clear information about timeline for homebuyer courses or how to enroll beyond a generic 'Learn More' link
Housing counseling contact is single staff member (Angelica), creating potential bottleneck for consultation scheduling
No online portal or intake system visible; all applications require phone contact or email
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Watertown needing down payment assistance and housing education
Watertown renters experiencing financial hardship and needing rent payment or financial counseling support
Owner-occupied homeowners in Jefferson County seeking residential rehabilitation funding
Residents facing mortgage default, creditor issues, or post-disaster financial recovery in the Watertown area
diagnosis: Neighbors of Watertown is the appropriate free non-profit resource for Watertown, NY residents and Jefferson County homeowners seeking HUD-approved housing counseling, down payment grants, or rehabilitation financing. The critical limitation is strict geographic focus—this organization serves only the Watertown area and is not a resource for consumers outside this region.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling for financial hardship and mortgage assistance
First-time homebuyer education program and courses
Down payment grants for qualified first-time homebuyers in Watertown
Historic property facade easement programs and downtown preservation
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Watertown, NY nonprofit offering HUD-approved housing counseling, first-time homebuyer assistance, and residential rehabilitation.
